reasoning: For China to mount an invasion of Taiwan involving ground forces before July 1, 2025 (less than two months away), it would require a massive, highly visible staging of amphibious and airborne forces over several months. Recent news reflects ongoing tension, military drills (like "Strait Thunder" in early April 2025), and coercion, but there are no reports of the logistical buildup necessary for an immediate invasion. The US has made no public accusation of an invasion, and doing so falsely or mistakenly is highly improbable. Given the lack of imminent indicators and the very short remaining timeframe, the probability of the US accusing China of a ground invasion by July 1, 2025 is functionally zero, so I am setting it to the minimum allowed value of 0.02.
